title: The Rise and Fall of the Third Reich
author: William L. Shirer
content: “The more enormous the lie, the more readily it is believed.”
action: Context: The observation of how mass-scale information manipulation overrides individual critical judgment.

title: The Gulag Archipelago
author: Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn
content: “The line separating good and evil passes not through states, nor between classes, nor between political parties either -- but right through every human heart.”
action: Context: The rejection of external system blame in favor of individual internal responsibility.

title: The Origins of Totalitarianism
author: Hannah Arendt
content: “The ideal subject of totalitarian rule is not the convinced Nazi or the convinced Communist, but people for whom the distinction between fact and fiction no longer exist.”
action: Context: The identification of truth-degradation as the primary prerequisite for systemic collapse.

title: Hiroshima
author: John Hersey
content: “There, in the tin factory, in the first moment of the atomic age, a human being was instantly turned into a shadow.”
action: Context: The record of the moment when industrial destructive capacity permanently outpaced human survival mechanisms.

author: George Orwell
content: “War is peace. Freedom is slavery. Ignorance is strength.”
action: Context: The documentation of linguistic inversion as a tool for enforcing total system control.
